# mlir-opt crashes in  llvm/include/llvm/Support/Casting.h:572

**Author:** Emilyaxe
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173377
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** crash, mlir:dataflow, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2026-01-17T08:11:45Z

## Body

When running opt on the following IR, mlir-opt crashes. 

version 2c02e4c7909a

test.mlir

```
func.func @trs(%idx1: index, %idx2: index, %s: f32) {
  scf.parallel (%i) = (%idx1) to (%idx2) step (%idx2) {
    %r = memref.alloca() : memref<10xf32>
    scf.forall (%e2) in (%idx2) {
      %a = memref.load %r[%idx2] : memref<10xf32>
    }
  }
  return
}

```


commads:

mlir-opt -remove-dead-values test.mlir

stacktrace

```
mlir-opt: llvm-project/llvm/include/llvm/Support/Casting.h:572: decltype(auto) llvm::cast(From *) [To = mlir::RegionBranchTerminatorOpInterface, From = mlir::Operation]: Assertion `isa<To>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace and instructions to reproduce the bug.
```

---

### Comment 2 - linuxlonelyeagle

The issue is same as https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173375 in my view. `remove-dead-values` crash is caused by scf.forall.in_parallel not implementing the RegionBranchTerminatorOpInterface, it looks like it also fails to implement the RegionBranchTerminatorOpInterface. However, even after I made the modifications locally, other bugs still exist. You can see. "I have a general understanding of the issue, so I've taken it upon myself to address it.
```
~ # mlir-opt b.mlir -test-liveness-analysis                                                                                                    
test_tag: for.all:
 operand #0: live
 region: #0:
   argument: #0: not live
module {
  func.func @trs(%arg0: index, %arg1: index, %arg2: f32) {
    scf.parallel (%arg3) = (%arg0) to (%arg1) step (%arg1) {
      %alloca = memref.alloca() : memref<10xf32>
      scf.forall (%arg4) in (%arg1) {
        %0 = memref.load %alloca[%arg1] : memref<10xf32>
      } {tag = "for.all"}
      scf.reduce 
    }
    return
  }
}
```

---

### Comment 3 - linuxlonelyeagle

fix by https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/174362 and https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/173505/files
